session_hint: the authentication token strategy for the user-facing web app
question: What did we ultimately go with for storing auth tokens in the web app?
turns: Plan for user auth: issue JWTs signed with RS256, store them in localStorage. Token contains user ID and role so the frontend can gate routes client-side without an extra API call.
JWT generation and verification middleware written. Frontend decodes the token to get the role. Works end to end in staging.
Security review flagged it: localStorage JWTs are fully readable by any injected script — classic XSS exfiltration vector. We cannot ship this.

We're now going with server-side sessions backed by Postgres, delivered via HttpOnly SameSite=Strict cookies. Session ID in cookie, all user data stays server-side. XSS cannot read HttpOnly cookies. JWT approach is dropped entirely. Final answer: session cookies.
